### Post by Lilszamora on 2008-09-01
When I first installed 8.04 the touchpad worked fine, then I started using a usb root mouse for a while and then the touchpad won't work anymore, I need it to start to work because of constraints on certain new surfaces that I use my labtop on.

Here's my Xorg.conf

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
```

### Post by prshah on 2008-09-01
> **Lilszamora said:**
> my Xorg.conf
```

<snip>
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
<snip>

```


The setup looks fine. Most HP touchpads have a switch along the top edge of the touchpad, to activate/ deactivate. Maybe you should try toggling that?

---

### Post by Lilszamora on 2008-09-01
> **prshah said:**
> The setup looks fine. Most HP touchpads have a switch along the top edge of the touchpad, to activate/ deactivate. Maybe you should try toggling that?

I've tried that, but for some reason it used to just open up help now it does nothing, I also checked under System -> Preferences -> Mouse, but it's all enabled, I have the touchpad options installed and enabled and everything.

### Post by sergiom99 on 2008-09-01

### Post by linux_tech on 2008-09-01
In terminal type-
```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
Try replacing your xorg.conf input device section with the following
```
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
	Option      	"SHMConfig" 		"on"
EndSection


```
[https://help.ubuntu.com/community/SynapticsTouchpad](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/SynapticsTouchpad)

---
